report_name: AI Model Discovers RCE Zero-Days in Vim and Emacs with Simple Prompts

attack_patterns.name: Develop Capabilities
attack_patterns.description: A security researcher used the Claude Code AI model to analyze the source code of Vim and GNU Emacs, which led to the discovery of novel zero-day remote code execution vulnerabilities.

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.description: An AI model from Anthropic used by a security researcher to analyze source code and discover zero-day vulnerabilities in Vim and GNU Emacs by providing it a simple prompt.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-34714
vulnerabilities.description: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in Vim, discovered by the Claude Code AI model. The flaw, related to missing security checks in the tabpanel sidebar feature, allows an attacker to execute arbitrary shell commands when a victim opens a malicious file. This vulnerability was patched in version 9.2.0272.

vulnerabilities.name: GNU Emacs RCE Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A potential remote code execution vulnerability in GNU Emacs discovered by the Claude Code AI model. The maintainers have disputed the finding, and the issue remains unpatched as of the report.

identities.name: Anthropic
identities.description: The company that developed the Claude Code AI model used to find the vulnerabilities in Vim and Emacs.

identities.name: Vim
identities.description: A widely used text editor whose source code was analyzed by an AI model, leading to the discovery and subsequent patching of a critical RCE vulnerability (CVE-2026-34714).

identities.name: GNU Emacs
identities.description: A widely used text editor in which an AI model discovered a potential RCE vulnerability. The finding is reportedly disputed by its maintainers and remains unpatched.

identities.name: Calif
identities.description: The AI red-teaming firm that employed the researcher who used an AI model to discover zero-day vulnerabilities.
